text.title: Weak-Model Preselection Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Weak filter ships with recall 0.80; strong model never sees the dropped items. Downstream task fails 20% more.
antipattern.root-cause: Author optimized precision (cheap noise reduction) at recall's expense.
antipattern.fix: Require recall ≥ 0.95 in CI. Tune threshold / few-shot to maximize recall first.

antipattern.symptom: Weak model filters out a 'low-priority' medical case that turned out to be urgent.
antipattern.root-cause: Cascade was deployed without distinguishing high-stakes from low-stakes routes.
antipattern.fix: Flag high_stakes=true → cascade falls back to ranker pattern (strong model sees all candidates ranked, not filtered).

antipattern.symptom: Weak model returns empty list on edge inputs; strong model receives nothing; user gets a non-answer.
antipattern.root-cause: Cascade designed for the happy path; edge cases silently produce empty outputs.
antipattern.fix: Always wire `escape_to_full_context=True` and route to it when confidence < threshold or output empty.

antipattern.symptom: Cascade reports 1.5x savings; operational overhead (extra calls, deploy complexity) eats the win.
antipattern.root-cause: Cargo-cult cascade on a low-noise route where single-pass would have been fine.
antipattern.fix: Require cost_savings_ratio ≥ 3x before deploying. Below that, single-pass strong model wins.
